visa_types.overview:
Overview
 This visa provides for the long stay of persons who will be full-time religious workers. Religious work is work of a religious nature for which the applicant has had relevant religious training. The religious work proposed must directly serve an organization’s religious objectives. It would usually be religious work involved in:

- Providing spiritual leadership
- Conducting worship
- Teaching or guidance in religion
- Ministering, pastoral care or proselytizing
- Other high level specialist work in relation to the above
EAST JURISDICTION : Applications from the residents of the States of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Orissa, Tripura, Sikkim and West Bengal, the Union Territory of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Nepal are to be decided by the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata.
The place of issue of a passport or the permanent resident mentioned in the passport does not determine the jurisdiction, rather it is the current place where an applicant resides for the past 6 months that determines whether he/she belongs to a specific jurisdiction.

All applicants claiming that they are working from home since last 6 months, for an employer outside the East Jurisdiction, will be requested to provide a declaration regarding the same on the organization letterhead, duly signed by the HR head and stating the current address of remote work and starting date of the remote work.
Please note that visa applications cannot be processed from applicants (including Indian nationals) not permanently residing in India. Those visiting India and wishing to go to Italy from India - even on their way back to their country of residence - need to apply for their National visa before departing from the relevant Consulate of their country of residence.
You’ll also need to know the documents that you’ll have to submit along with your application, how long the application might take and fees you’ll have to pay.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

The Family reunion visas must be authorized through the specified authorization ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authorization in which are listed the family members who may apply for the visa.

Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

EAST JURISDICTION : Applications from the residents of the States of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Orissa, Tripura, Sikkim and West Bengal, the Union Territory of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Nepal are to be decided by the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata.
In the case of applicants who are minors, both parents must be accompanying them at the time of submission.
The place of issue of a passport or the permanent resident mentioned in the passport does not determine the jurisdiction, rather it is the current place where an applicant resides for the past 6 months that determines whether he/she belongs to a specific jurisdiction.

All applicants claiming that they are working from home since last 6 months, for an employer outside the East Jurisdiction, will be requested to provide a declaration regarding the same on the organization letterhead, duly signed by the HR head and stating the current address of remote work and starting date of the remote work.
Please note that visa applications cannot be processed from applicants (including Indian nationals) not permanently residing in India. Those visiting India and wishing to go to Italy from India - even on their way back to their country of residence - need to apply for their Schengen visa before departing from the relevant Consulate of their country of residence.

You’ll also need to know the documents that you’ll have to submit along with your application, how long the application might take and fees you’ll have to pay.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

Overview

This visa provides for the temporary stay of persons who will be full-time religious workers in Schengen. - Ministering, pastoral care or proselytizing

visa_types.overview:
Short-stay visas are for visits of up to a total of 90 days, calculated over a period of 180 days. The number of days is calculated from the date of first entry to the territory of the countries of the Schengen area. In case of transit visas, the duration is authorized in accordance with the period of stay required for the transit.

Important Notice

Approved list of Indian Travel Insurance Companies of the consular posts of Schengen States
Providing travel medical insurances for Schengen visa procedure

Please note that the travel insurance has to be minimum 10 days if your duration of trip is less than 10 days. Further if the duration is more than 10 days then need to purchase travel insurance covering the entire duration of stay in the Schengen area.

Please click here  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/11PloI9ddNtgZgXMIg8eSQ/014b7b77f11444d3c9764bcde8ed1e86/Approved-TMI-Companies-List-Apr-2026.pdf)for the updated Travel Insurance List

Visa applicants are advised to note that travel medical insurances issued by Indian insurance companies other than the ones listed above will not be accepted for Schengen visa procedure.

However, visa applicants may seek to obtain insurance from Italy only where claims against the insurance company would be recoverable in a Schengen State. 

After scrutinizing the visa application by Consulate General of Kolkata, your application may have the following

- Visa applied for is granted.
- Further documentation is requested.
- Applicant is requested to appear for a personal interview.
- The application is rejected.
Short stay visas are processed in Kolkata by:

Consulate General of Italy, Kolkata
5G New Road Alipore
Kolkata 700027

Short stay visa applications cannot be submitted more than 180 days in advance before the proposed date of travel.

However, they must be submitted at least 15 working days prior to the proposed date of travel. VFS Global is the outsourcing office responsible for the reception and release of passports for short stay visa applications. The Processing time for East jurisdiction is minimum 15 Working days for short term application. The Processing time may vary depending on the Consulate General of Italy, Kolkata. 

APPLICATION PICK-UP SERVICE FOR VISA SUBMISSION
You can avail of the Application Pick-Up Service for submitting your application – C-type/short-term visa (only if you have already applied for an Italian visa at the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata within the last 59 months). For more information about this service, the applicant will have to email VFS at postal_italy_kol@vfsglobal.com. (mailto:postal_italy_kol@vfsglobal.com)
STEPS TO FOLLOW:
1. Applicants need to email us at postal_italy_kol@vfsglobal.com, (mailto:postal_italy_kol@vfsglobal.com) mentioning the following details:
- Jurisdiction
- Pick-up address
- Visa category
- Applicant’s information
- Passport copy
2. VFS to send the details to the Consulate and once all information is validated by the Consulate General of Italy, applicant will receive a payment link via email. Upon payment, we will arrange for the pick-up of the application at the desired location.
3. The applicant must complete the visa application form, ensuring it is duly signed, and gather all supporting documents as per category visa checklist and 2 photographs that meet specifications. These have to be 

enclosed along with the passport in an envelope.
4. Each application must be packed in a separate envelope.
PLEASE NOTE: as the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata can only retrieve an applicant's fingerprints once, the postal service is only available for those applicants who are applying for an Italian visa for their second time in a period of 59 months; if you have already applied twice for an Italian visa within this period of time, you will not be eligible for the service

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa option is for students who wish to undergo Internship in Italy.
EAST JURISDICTION : Applications from the residents of the States of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Orissa, Tripura, Sikkim and West Bengal, the Union Territory of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Nepal are to be decided by the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata.
The place of issue of a passport or the permanent resident mentioned in the passport does not determine the jurisdiction, rather it is the current place where an applicant resides for the past 6 months that determines whether he/she belongs to a specific jurisdiction.

All applicants claiming that they are working from home since last 6 months, for an employer outside the East Jurisdiction, will be requested to provide a declaration regarding the same on the organization letterhead, duly signed by the HR head and stating the current address of remote work and starting date of the remote work.

Please note that visa applications cannot be processed from applicants (including Indian nationals) not permanently residing in India. Those visiting India and wishing to go to Italy from India - even on their way back to their country of residence - need to apply for their Schengen visa before departing from the relevant Consulate of their country of residence.
You’ll also need to know the documents that you’ll have to submit along with your application, how long the application might take and fees you’ll have to pay.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is for professional and amateur sportspeople, judges and adjudicators who want to come to Italy to participate in their field of sport.
EAST JURISDICTION : Applications from the residents of the States of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Orissa, Tripura, Sikkim and West Bengal, the Union Territory of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Nepal are to be decided by the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata.
In the case of applicants who are minors, both parents must be accompanying them at the time of submission.
The place of issue of a passport or the permanent resident mentioned in the passport does not determine the jurisdiction, rather it is the current place where an applicant resides for the past 6 months that determines whether he/she belongs to a specific jurisdiction.

All applicants claiming that they are working from home since last 6 months, for an employer outside the East Jurisdiction, will be requested to provide a declaration regarding the same on the organization letterhead, duly signed by the HR head and stating the current address of remote work and starting date of the remote work.
Please note that visa applications cannot be processed from applicants (including Indian nationals) not permanently residing in India. Those visiting India and wishing to go to Italy from India - even on their way back to their country of residence - need to apply for their Schengen visa before departing from the relevant Consulate of their country of residence.

You’ll also need to know the documents that you’ll have to submit along with your application, how long the application might take and fees you’ll have to pay.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

[ita > ind > en > Kolkata > Short term fee]

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Family reunion visas are visas intended to reunite family members specifically husbands/wives, children and parents economically dependent on the sponsors. It is a long term (type D) period visa. The Family reunion visas must be authorized through the specified authorization ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authorization in which are listed the family members who may apply for the visa.
Please note: the family members have to provide documentary evidence about the family ties which will be formally and substantially verified by the Embassy of Italy.
Please be informed that your Nulla Osta is valid for 6 months from the date of issue. Your application will be accepted only with a valid Nulla Osta.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

  The Visa allows applicants to enter Italy for a short term period (type "C") or for a long term period (type "D") with the purpose to work while earning a salary.
This Visas are subject to an Authorization ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authority ("SPORTELLO UNICO PER L'IMMIGRAZIONE") which is valid within 6 months from the date of issuing
EAST JURISDICTION : Applications from the residents of the States of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Orissa, Tripura, Sikkim and West Bengal, the Union Territory of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Nepal are to be decided by the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata.
The place of issue of a passport or the permanent resident mentioned in the passport does not determine the jurisdiction, rather it is the current place where an applicant resides for the past 6 months that determines whether he/she belongs to a specific jurisdiction.

All applicants claiming that they are working from home since last 6 months, for an employer outside the East Jurisdiction, will be requested to provide a declaration regarding the same on the organization letterhead, duly signed by the HR head and stating the current address of remote work and starting date of the remote work.
Please note that visa applications cannot be processed from applicants (including Indian nationals) not permanently residing in India. Those visiting India and wishing to go to Italy from India - even on their way back to their country of residence - need to apply for their Schengen visa before departing from the relevant Consulate of their country of residence.
You’ll also need to know the documents that you’ll have to submit along with your application, how long the application might take and fees you’ll have to pay.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

## Overview
  The Visa allows applicants to enter Italy for a short term period (type "C") or for a long term period (type "D") with the purpose to work while earning a salary.
This Visas are subject to an Authorization ("NULLA OSTA") issued by the competent Italian Authority ("SPORTELLO UNICO PER L'IMMIGRAZIONE") which is valid within 6 months from the date of issuing
EAST JURISDICTION : Applications from the residents of the States of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Orissa, Tripura, Sikkim and West Bengal, the Union Territory of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Nepal are to be decided by the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata.
The place of issue of a passport or the permanent resident mentioned in the passport does not determine the jurisdiction, rather it is the current place where an applicant resides for the past 6 months that determines whether he/she belongs to a specific jurisdiction.

All applicants claiming that they are working from home since last 6 months, for an employer outside the East Jurisdiction, will be requested to provide a declaration regarding the same on the organization letterhead, duly signed by the HR head and stating the current address of remote work and starting date of the remote work.
Please note that visa applications cannot be processed from applicants (including Indian nationals) not permanently residing in India. Those visiting India and wishing to go to Italy from India - even on their way back to their country of residence - need to apply for their Schengen visa before departing from the relevant Consulate of their country of residence.
You’ll also need to know the documents that you’ll have to submit along with your application, how long the application might take and fees you’ll have to pay.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.

## Visa Fees

[ita > ind > en > Kolkata > Long Term fee]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid by cash or Demand Draft /Cash/Debit & CREDIT Card in Indian Rupees (INR). Demand Draft should be drawn in favour of “VFS Global Services Pvt Ltd”, payable at Kolkata, India. The Visa Fee is subject to change without notice

'The Visa Fee is subject to change without notice, and the exchange rate is given by the Mission'.

VFS SERVICE CHARGE

In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge (inclusive of Goods & Service Tax –SGST @9% and CGST@9%), of INR 652 (Payable in Cash/Online/Debit/Credit card).
To add value to our customer, we offer some value-added services. You will need to pay an extra fee in addition to the visa application fee when you avail these value-added services.
Courier Facility available at INR 912/- (inclusive of Goods & Service Tax –SGST @9% and CGST@9%) per application.
Courier Assurance Facility available at INR 782/- (inclusive of Goods & Service Tax –SGST @9% and CGST@9%) per application.
Premium Lounge Facility available at INR 5734/- (inclusive of Goods & Service Tax –SGST @9% and CGST@9%) per application.
Note: Visa fee and service charges are not refunded if your application is refused by the Consulate General of Italy, or if you withdraw you application while it is under processing at the Consulate General of Italy, Kolkata.

## Documents Required
For more information regarding documents please click the below link. 
Please Click here for document required list. (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/41kEgSFbOGSfB2JKoKzkmz/5adfba86aea74d085eaa7ab41eef473c/Updated-employment-visa-new.pdf)
Besides the listed documents, please note that applicants working remotely from an employer outside of East Jurisdiction must provide an HR letter on company letterhead confirming that the applicant works remotely, specifying the address of remote work and date of commencement of the remote work, duly signed by the HR head.
Please note that the travel insurance has to be for one month from the date of arrival in the Schengen area.
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ind/en/ita/travel-insurance)
List of insurance companies approved for Schengen visa procedure (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3MwvMBiJkCQWPS86ZXTF8A/2ed2f58ace7df5f584523a9dc28600c5/Approved-TMI-Companies-List-Apr-2026.pdf)

## Processing Time
You should apply for your visa between 15 and 180 working days before your planned departure date.
Processing time for the Long-term visa is minimum 30 days. The Processing time may vary depending on the Consulate General of Italy Kolkata.

## Application Forms
Visa Application Form 

Please submit the Information General Data Protection Regulation mandatory with every application(with or without biometrics; for applicants under 18 years of age both parents need to sign) 

Visa declaration for Applicants with processing time less than 15 working days 
Download Instructions:

The forms are in PDF format.
You will need an Adobe Acrobat Reader to view and download these forms.

To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link 
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer. 
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link. 
- Select Save Target As from the list 
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad/Remote worker Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
For more information regarding documents please click the below link.
Please Click here for document required list
 
Besides the listed documents, please note that applicants working remotely from an employer outside of East Jurisdiction must provide an HR letter on company letterhead confirming that the applicant works remotely, specifying the address of remote work and date of commencement of the remote work, duly signed by the HR head.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS FOR REMOTE WORKERS ONLY
Applicant for a remote-work visa, in addition to the aforesaid requirements, must submit also:
- the employment or collaboration contract or the related binding offer to carry out a work activity that requires the possession of one of the requirements set out in Article 27 – quater, paragraph 1, of Legislative Decree No. 286 of 25 July 1998. The amount of the annual salary, as derived from the employment contract or from the binding offer, must not be lower than the salary provided for in the national collective agreements entered into by trade unions that are comparatively more representative at national level, and in any case not less than the average gross annual salary as recorded by ISTAT and available at the following link: Principali aggregati annuali di Contabilità Nazionale : Valori pro capite (istat.it)), and
- 

- a declaration signed by the employer, accompanied by a copy of a valid identification document, certifying the absence of convictions against him, in the last 5 years, for offences referred to in Article 22, paragraph 5-bis, of the Consolidated Text. Italian Authorities will carry out random checks on these declarations through Italian Police Headquarters.

RESIDENCE PERMIT ONCE IN ITALY
You must apply for a residence permit (permesso di soggiorno) directly at the Questura (police headquarters) of the Municipality in which you reside as digital nomad or remote-work employee, within eight working days of your arrival into Italian territory, even in the case of a visa lasting less than 90 days.
For the purposes of applying for a residency permit, you must submit to the Italian Questura the same original documentation you used to apply for your visa, stamped by the Italian diplomatic/consular mission.
Please note that the travel insurance has to be for one month from the date of arrival in the Schengen area.
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/ind/en/ita/travel-insurance)
List of insurance companies approved for Schengen visa procedure (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3MwvMBiJkCQWPS86ZXTF8A/2ed2f58ace7df5f584523a9dc28600c5/Approved-TMI-Companies-List-Apr-2026.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The digital nomad / remote worker visa is intended for individuals who are not EU citizens and who intend to work remotely while living in Italy.
The visa is only available to highly specialized workers whose careers require or exceed the requirements listed in article 27-quater of the Legislative Decree n. 286 of 25 July 1998, including careers that require post-secondary degrees or at least three years’ professional training or experience.
The visa is subdivided into two categories: “digital nomads”, who are freelancers, consultants, or other independent specialists, and “remote workers”, who are employees of a company but can perform their work completely remotely
EAST JURISDICTION : Applications from the residents of the States of Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Orissa, Tripura, Sikkim and West Bengal, the Union Territory of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands and Nepal are to be decided by the Consulate of Italy in Kolkata.
The place of issue of a passport or the permanent resident mentioned in the passport does not determine the jurisdiction, rather it is the current place where an applicant resides for the past 6 months that determines whether he/she belongs to a specific jurisdiction.
All applicants claiming that they are working from home since last 6 months, for an employer outside the East Jurisdiction, will be requested to provide a declaration regarding the same on the organization letterhead, duly signed by the HR head and stating the current address of remote work and starting date of the remote work.
Please note that visa applications cannot be processed from applicants (including Indian nationals) not permanently residing in India. Those visiting India and wishing to go to Italy from India - even on their way back to their country of residence - need to apply for their National visa before departing from the relevant Consulate of their country of residence.
You’ll also need to know the documents that you’ll have to submit along with your application, how long the application might take and fees you’ll have to pay.
Your visa application must be submitted in the country where you have continuously lived for the last 6 months, and you will need to provide proof of this residence when you apply. Please note that we cannot process visa applications from visitors to India, even if you are an Indian national. If you are visiting India and plan to travel to Italy, you must apply for your Schengen visa in your home country before your trip to India.
Your passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended visa duration and must have been issued within the last 10 years.
